evals.description: Fresh project with a package.json but no README and no docs/. Skill should create a minimal README and at least a getting-started page.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-doc-0 && cd /tmp/eval-doc-0 && git init && echo '{"name":"acme-cli","version":"1.0.0","description":"A CLI tool for managing deployments","bin":{"acme":"./bin/acme.js"},"scripts":{"test":"jest","start":"node bin/acme.js"}}' > package.json && mkdir -p bin src && echo '#!/usr/bin/env node
console.log("acme");' > bin/acme.js
evals.expected_output: README.md created with a brief description and Quick Start section. docs/getting-started.md created with numbered installation and first-run steps. README links to docs pages.

assertions.text: README.md was created and is non-empty
README.md contains a '## Quick Start' section
README.md contains at least one markdown link pointing to a file inside docs/
docs/getting-started.md was created
docs/getting-started.md contains numbered steps (lines starting with '1.', '2.', etc.)
README.md does not contain a long feature list — the description section is 3 sentences or fewer before the Quick Start heading

evals.description: Project has an existing README with content but no docs/ directory. Skill should preserve existing README content and create a docs/ structure.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-doc-1 && cd /tmp/eval-doc-1 && git init && echo '{"name":"mylib","version":"2.3.1","description":"A utility library for data transformation"}' > package.json && printf '# mylib

A utility library for data transformation.

## Installation

```bash
npm install mylib
```

## Usage

Import and call `transform(data)`.
' > README.md
evals.expected_output: Existing README.md content is preserved. A docs/ directory is created with at least getting-started.md. README is updated to link to the new docs pages. Existing Installation section is not duplicated.

assertions.text: README.md still contains the original 'A utility library for data transformation' description
At least one file was created inside a docs/ directory
README.md contains at least one markdown link pointing to a docs/ page
The word 'Installation' appears only once in README.md (existing section not duplicated)
docs/getting-started.md contains a link back to README.md

evals.description: Minimal project — a single Python script with no complex structure. Skill should choose a minimal doc set and not create placeholder pages.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-doc-2 && cd /tmp/eval-doc-2 && git init && printf '[project]
name = "dedupe"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Remove duplicate lines from a file"
' > pyproject.toml && printf '#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    seen = set()
    for line in sys.stdin:
        if line not in seen:
            print(line, end="")
            seen.add(line)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
' > dedupe.py
evals.expected_output: README.md and minimal docs created (getting-started.md at most). No architecture.md, contributing.md, or deployment.md created for such a simple project. No pages with TBD or placeholder content.

assertions.text: README.md was created and is non-empty
No created file contains 'TBD', 'TODO', or 'Coming soon' as its only body content
docs/architecture.md was NOT created (project is too simple to warrant it)
README.md or docs/getting-started.md shows how to run the script (e.g. a code block with 'python' or 'dedupe')

evals.description: README has a badge pointing to a CI workflow file that does not exist. Skill must flag the stale badge but must NOT silently remove it.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-doc-3/.github/workflows && cd /tmp/eval-doc-3 && git init && echo '{"name":"svc","version":"1.0.0"}' > package.json && echo 'name: build
on: push
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3' > .github/workflows/build.yml && printf '# svc

[![CI](https://github.com/org/svc/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/org/svc/actions/workflows/ci.yml)

A service.
' > README.md
evals.expected_output: Skill flags that the badge references ci.yml but only build.yml exists. The badge remains in README.md untouched. A warning or note is printed to the user about the mismatch.

assertions.text: The original badge markdown is still present in README.md (the img.shields.io or actions badge line was not deleted)
The skill output or summary mentions that the badge references a workflow file (ci.yml) that does not exist
The skill suggests updating the badge to reference build.yml instead of ci.yml

evals.description: Project already has README and docs/ pages, but they lack cross-links between pages. Skill should add missing breadcrumbs and See Also sections without rewriting existing content.
evals.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval-doc-4/docs && cd /tmp/eval-doc-4 && git init && echo '{"name":"platform","version":"3.0.0","description":"A deployment platform"}' > package.json && printf '# platform

A deployment platform.

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md)
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)
' > README.md && printf '# Getting Started

1. Install: `npm install -g platform`
2. Run: `platform deploy`
' > docs/getting-started.md && printf '# Architecture

The platform consists of a CLI, an API server, and a worker queue.
' > docs/architecture.md
evals.expected_output: docs/getting-started.md and docs/architecture.md are updated to include breadcrumb links back to README and See Also links to related pages. Existing content in both files is preserved.

assertions.text: docs/getting-started.md still contains the original numbered install and run steps
docs/getting-started.md contains a link back to ../README.md or README.md
docs/architecture.md contains a link back to ../README.md or README.md
At least one docs page links to another docs page (e.g. getting-started links to architecture or vice versa)
